text: Good morning Baker family! Amara's school play is Saturday at 10am — Mount Zion fellowship hall. Don't be late, Daddy 😄
We'll be there! Isaiah is so excited to see his big sister up on stage.
Wouldn't miss it for the world. Me and Mama will be front row.
Wish I could make it. Give Amara a big hug from Uncle Curtis and the kids.
Also — building committee meeting at church is moved to Wednesday 6pm. Daddy, Deacon Pittman said to remind you.
Got it. Already on the calendar. Thank Marcus for the heads up.
Jaylen and Destiny want to know if Grandpa is bringing his famous potato salad to the Juneteenth cookout 🥗
You tell those babies yes. Grandma already bought the eggs.
John you still at the garden? What time you want supper?
Finishing up the tomatoes. Be home by noon. Don't fuss.
Pick up a loaf of bread on the way. The good kind from the gas station bakery.
Yes ma'am.
😂 Love you. Don't forget we have prayer circle at Gloria's at 7.
Daddy, I'm sending you a link. Click it to see photos from Amara's recital last month. You just tap the blue words.
I tapped it and it opened up. Those pictures are something else. She looks just like you did at that age.
Ha! Mama says the same thing. Good job figuring out the link, Daddy 👏
Don't act so surprised. Your old man has got a few tricks.
Curtis you still coming down next weekend? Skeeter says the speckled trout are running good off the pier.
Yes sir, plan is Friday evening. Me and Jaylen both. He's been practicing his casting in the backyard all week.
That boy is going to outfish us both. I'll have the gear ready. Mama's already planning the fry-up.
Tell her not to worry, we'll catch enough. See you Friday, Pop.
Hey little brother. How's things in Biloxi? Doreen doing okay?
All good here. Garden's coming in nice. Doreen's running the prayer circle over at Gloria Tate's tonight. Same as always.
Good to hear. I'll try to come down for the Fourth if the car holds up. Tell Doreen I said hello.
John you see them deer got into my collard greens again last night? Third time this month.
Told you to put that chicken wire up before spring. Come by and I'll help you sort it out this afternoon.
Coffee on the porch with Skeeter this morning, watched the street wake up.
Cut the shelves for Janelle's garage today — oak for honesty.
Walked the raised beds with my coffee; tomatoes and okra coming in.
Building committee meets first Saturday, 8 AM — painting day in November.
Sunday table: collards, cornbread, smothered chicken, peach cobbler.
Called Harold; we'll see each other at the family gathering.
Curtis fished the bayou with me — more about the company than the catch.
Amara and Isaiah helped in the workshop, small tasks and big grins.
Gospel and blues on the workshop radio, Sam Cooke when the mood needs it.
Neighborhood loop walk at 6:30; the knees complain in the damp.
Grilled on the Weber this weekend, cheated on the sodium a little.
CPAP data review with Dr. Pham in November — hate the mask but wear it.
Built a gabled birdhouse, cedar for the smell, gave it away.
Doreen and I bickered about the thermostat, agreed on everything else.
Sun Herald over morning coffee, two cups black by 6.
Home Depot run for saw blades — brand-loyal and slow to buy.
Tithe to Shiloh Baptist; faith the quiet backbone of the week.
Braves on cable tonight, the Saints come Sunday.
Made Mama's gumbo — the weight of home in a pot.
Grandkids over for the weekend, garden and workshop lessons.
Roof's held fine since Zeta, AC newer, the truck runs clean.
Waffle House Saturday, the usual order.
Fixed the porch rocker; the rockers face the street on purpose.
Okra because Mama grew it, herbs by the back door.
